codeReview.summary: Implementation provides AlertManager with loop detection, consecutive failures, cost threshold, and policy violation monitoring. The code is well-structured with proper error handling, configurable thresholds, and reasonable defaults. Unit tests cover all major functionality.

issues.description: Policy violation alerts reuse AlertType::ValidationFailureRateHigh instead of a semantically appropriate alert type. While functional, this makes it harder to distinguish policy violations from validation failures in alerts history. Consider adding a dedicated AlertType variant like PolicyViolation.

The cost warning condition (line 508) overlaps with the critical condition (line 503) when task_cost equals max_cost_per_task. Both warning and critical branches could fire on the same call, though the cooldown prevents duplicate alerts of the same type. Logic could be clearer with explicit ordering.

task_costs HashMap grows unbounded - remove_task_cost exists but is never called automatically when a task completes or is cancelled. The remove_task_cost only removes the per-task cost but keeps total_cost intact (which is correct), but there is no lifecycle management for when tasks are removed from the system.

task_failures HashMap grows unbounded - record_task_success removes the task entry, but if a task is cancelled without a success or if it fails but is never cleaned up, the entry remains. No remove_task_failures method exists.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The transcript shows the worker explored ReactLoopState in state_machine.rs but implemented LoopDetectionState independently in alerts.rs. No shared state documentation explains the relationship between these two loop tracking mechanisms or why they coexist as separate implementations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows worker searched for 'ReactLoopState' but final implementation uses LoopDetectionState in alerts.rs. Handoff mentions 'ReAct loop' but doesn't address integration with existing state_machine loop tracking.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the Rust skill procedure correctly (cargo check before clippy, crate-scoped validation). No convention violations detected.
The Rust skill was used but didn't provide guidance on alert system design patterns or integration points. Worker had to infer how alerts should integrate with the broader orchestrator flow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker created alerts.rs from scratch with no reference to how AlertManager should be used by the orchestrator or state machine.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered that AlertType needed the Hash trait to be used in HashMap (last_alert_time). This is implementation knowledge that wasn't documented anywhere.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit shows 'Add Hash trait to AlertType for HashMap usage' as a change to metrics.rs

summary: The observability-alerts feature passes review. The implementation is functionally complete, compiles cleanly, passes all 8 unit tests and clippy checks. Non-blocking issues are limited to semantic choices (reusing AlertType for policy violations) and missing cleanup methods for HashMap entries. The most notable gap is the lack of documented relationship between the new LoopDetectionState and any existing ReactLoopState in the codebase - this should be recorded in shared state for future maintainers.
